concept: Rub / Work with Friction / Stroke Repeatedly with Pressure
description: To rub: warmth into cold hands, oil into wood, the ache from a shoulder. Repeated pressured strokes — friction as care. Firmer than 'hewe' (wipe), warmer than 'peki' (squeeze).
sound_symbolism: The rolling 'r' is the repetition itself, 'hi' the heat-breath friction makes. The word warms.
grammatical_notes: Transitive: 'mia thumai rohi.' (I rub my fingers.) Into: 'shia loesi muo wolea rohi.' (They rub oil into the wood.) For an ache: 'mia talua rohi.' (I rub my shoulder.) Contrast 'hewe' (wipe), which cleans a surface; 'rohi' works by friction into the thing.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Warmth rubbed into a tired limb is compassion at skin level — care that needs no words to be understood.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Fire from friction, shine from friction, warmth from friction — rubbing was heat before matches and medicine before pills.
